data.title: Cigna on Track for Largest Percent Increase Since December 2024 — Data Talk
data.description:
Cigna Group (CI) is currently at $312.75, up $15.77 or 5.31%

  • Would be highest close since July 7, 2025, when it closed at $313.63
  • On pace for largest percent increase since Dec. 18, 2024, when it rose 6.33%
  • Currently up eight of the past nine days
  • Currently up six consecutive days; up 10.1% over this period
  • Longest winning streak since Aug. 20, 2025, when it rose for 10 straight trading days
  • Best six day stretch since the six days ending Feb. 8, 2024, when it rose 10.41%
  • Up 9.29% this week; best weekly performance since the week ending Dec. 15, 2023, when it rose 12.25%
  • Up 13.26% year-to-date; on pace for best year since 2022, when it rose 44.29%
  • Down 14.75% from its all-time closing high of $366.85 on Sept. 16, 2024
  • Down 8.47% from 52 weeks ago (Oct. 4, 2024), when it closed at $341.70
  • Down 12.78% from its 52-week closing high of $358.58 on Oct. 16, 2024
  • Up 19.27% from its 52-week closing low of $262.23 on Aug. 1, 2025
  • Traded as high as $315.47; highest intraday level since July 7, 2025, when it hit $320.92
  • Up 6.23% at today's intraday high; largest intraday percent increase since Dec. 18, 2024, when it rose as much as 7.04%
  • Second best performer in the S&P 500 today

All data as of 2:40:16 PM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: Abu Dhabi-Backed Wealth Manager Corient Buys Its Fifth Multibillion Firm in 30 Days — Barrons.com
data.description:
By David Wignall

Now backed by an Abu Dhabi sovereign-wealth fund, Corient is opening its wallet. The wealth management firm revealed yesterday that it has bought Bristlecone Advisors, a multifamily office with roughly $2 billion in client assets.

The transaction extends Corient's recent shopping spree, which has already seen the firm announce the acquisition of four multibillion-dollar firms in the past month. In September, it bought two firms in New England with $8 billion in combined client assets. The company has also revealed plans to buy two European wealth managers with a total of $214 billion in assets.

If all those deals close, Corient's total assets under advisement will soar to $430 billion.

Its latest acquisition is Bristlecone. Founded in 1999, Bristlecone is a 19-person team based in Bellevue, Wa., according to regulatory documents. The firm serves roughly 400 clients and employs eight registered advisors.

Kevin S. Berry, Bristlecone's managing partner, says the buyout gives his team "additional services, scale, and resources" to bulk out their services and operational capabilities. Terms of the transaction weren't disclosed.

While Corient's recent growth has been explosive, the firm's longer history is more mixed. At the start of the decade, Corient's parent company, Toronto-based CI Financial, bought a slew of American wealth managers. But CI soon accumulated a heavy debt load, prompting the Canadian firm to sell a minority stake of its U.S. wealth business in 2023. CI ultimately sold itself to a unit of the Abu Dhabi sovereign-wealth fund in 2024.

The third quarter of 2025 was a historic period for mergers and acquisitions in the wealth management industry, according to a report by DeVoe & Company. The consulting and investment banking firm counted 94 transactions from July to September, putting the industry on track for the hottest M&A year on record.

"PE-backed consolidators represent the majority of activity, fueling the industry's consolidation," DeVoe reported.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
